categories.label: Earnings & Financial Results
categories.description: Quarterly earnings reports, revenue/EPS figures, profit margins, guidance, dividends, capex forecasts, and any article reporting specific financial results vs expectations.

categories.label: Market & Sector Sentiment
categories.description: Broad market roundups, sector rotation, macro data mentions, index movements, and multi-stock earnings previews where META is mentioned alongside peers.

categories.label: Analyst & Consensus Signals
categories.description: Analyst upgrades/downgrades, price target changes, buy/sell ratings, and institutional positioning commentary on META.

categories.label: AI Strategy & Investment
categories.description: Development and deployment of AI models (Llama, Meta AI, Movie Gen), AI infrastructure buildout, custom chips, open-source AI strategy, AI partnerships, and generative AI product launches.

categories.label: Regulatory, Antitrust & Compliance
categories.description: EU Digital Markets Act and Digital Services Act proceedings, antitrust fines, FTC actions, data privacy rulings, GDPR enforcement, and government investigations into Meta's business practices.

categories.label: Child Safety & Content Moderation
categories.description: Lawsuits and legislation targeting youth social media use, teen account protections, sextortion enforcement, content moderation staffing, Senate hearings on child exploitation, and platform safety features.

categories.label: Hardware, AR/VR & Metaverse
categories.description: Quest headsets, Orion AR glasses, Reality Labs initiatives, EssilorLuxottica smart glasses partnership, mixed reality ecosystem, and VR/AR market developments.

categories.label: Platform & Product Updates
categories.description: New features for Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, Threads, and Messenger including creator tools, business integrations, advertising features, content deals, and service outages.

categories.label: Geopolitical & Government Relations
categories.description: Country-level bans or blocks (Turkey, Russia, Brazil), government content removal requests, election integrity measures, foreign interference takedowns, and international policy disputes.

dimensions.scale: 1=routine operational update, minor product tweak, or corporate filing with no revenue/margin implications, 10=event directly and substantially impacts revenue streams, cost structure, or total addressable market
1=outcome fully expected or information already widely known, 10=completely unexpected revelation that contradicts prior consensus or assumptions
1=transient event affecting only the next few days, 10=structural shift with implications lasting years
1=neutral or balanced tone with no clear positive/negative lean, 10=extremely strong positive or negative tone throughout the article
1=pure opinion, speculation, or vague commentary with no specific data, 10=rich in hard data, specific figures, named sources, and quantified outcomes
1=ambiguous implications that could be interpreted as positive or negative, 10=unambiguously good or bad for the company with no reasonable counter-interpretation
1=affects only this company specifically, 5=sector-wide implications, 10=broad macro or market-wide event
1=no regulatory or legal angle whatsoever, 10=major legal ruling, regulatory fine, or compliance mandate with direct financial or operational consequences
1=no leadership involvement or strategic implication, 10=major CEO/executive-level strategic announcement or leadership change signaling new direction
1=reinforces the prevailing investment thesis and existing narrative about the company, 10=fundamentally challenges or redirects the market's understanding of the company's trajectory
1=rehash or follow-up of previously reported information with no new details, 10=first report of genuinely new information not previously in the public domain
1=no effect on competitive positioning, 10=major shift in competitive dynamics through partnerships, market entry, or rival disruption
1=no financial results reported or results exactly match expectations, 10=reported figures dramatically exceed or miss prior guidance and analyst expectations as described in the article
1=transient story that resolves or fades within a day, 10=persistent story that remains relevant and develops over weeks or months

kept.materiality: Ranges from Form 4 filings and minor feature announcements (Instagram stickers, WhatsApp filters) to $1.4B settlements, EU antitrust charges, and quarterly earnings with major capex guidance — wide variation across the corpus.
kept.surprise: Spans from scheduled product launches and routine regulatory requests to unexpected events like the Q1 2024 16% share plunge on capex guidance, the first-ever dividend declaration, and the $1.4B Texas facial recognition settlement.
kept.temporal_horizon: Ranges from one-day service outages and single-day market roundups to structural shifts like the EU DMA compliance model, Orion AR glasses prototype revealing a decade-long hardware strategy, and open-source AI positioning.
kept.sentiment_strength: Varies from neutral corporate blog posts and Form 4 filings to strongly negative articles about child exploitation hearings and strongly positive articles about earnings beats and dividend initiation.
kept.information_density: Ranges from one-line headline fragments and opinion pieces to detailed earnings transcripts with specific revenue, EPS, margin figures, and analyst price targets with precise numbers.
kept.directional_clarity: Some articles are clearly positive (earnings beat, dividend initiation) or clearly negative (EU antitrust charges, $1.4B fine), while others are ambiguous (AI spending increase could be positive long-term but negative short-term).
kept.scope: Ranges from META-only events like Threads feature updates to sector-wide Big Tech regulatory crackdowns and broad macro articles about market selloffs and central bank decisions.
kept.regulatory_risk: Highly variable — from zero regulatory content in product announcements to major EU DMA charges, $1.4B Texas settlement, FTC privacy actions, and multiple country-level investigations.
kept.management_signal: Ranges from no leadership angle in routine news to Zuckerberg's Senate apology, strategic AI reorganization announcements, open-source AI vision letters, and Connect keynote product reveals.
kept.narrative_shift: Most articles reinforce existing narratives (AI investment, regulatory pressure), but some shift the thesis — first dividend signals capital return era, Q1 capex guidance sparks AI spending fears, Orion prototype reframes hardware ambitions.
kept.repeatedness: Clear variation between first-break stories (dividend announcement, Orion reveal, Texas settlement) and repeated coverage of the same events across multiple outlets or follow-up articles days later.
kept.competitive_impact: Ranges from no competitive angle in regulatory filings to major competitive moves like Llama 3 challenging OpenAI, EssilorLuxottica partnership deepening, Movie Gen rivaling OpenAI's Sora, and potential Apple AI integration.
kept.financial_result_surprise: Most articles contain no financial results at all, but earnings articles show clear variation — Q4 2023 EPS tripled expectations and shares surged 17%, while Q1 2024 guidance disappointed and shares plunged 16%.
kept.expected_duration: Ranges from service outages resolving in hours and single-day market roundups to ongoing regulatory sagas (EU DMA proceedings spanning months) and structural product strategies (AR glasses, open-source AI).

dropped.controversy: Most META articles in this corpus do not show meaningful investor divisiveness — events tend to be clearly positive or negative rather than divisive among investors. The few controversial items (content moderation decisions, Palestine content) overlap heavily with directional_clarity and would score uniformly low for investor-relevant controversy.
dropped.actionability: Nearly all material news in this corpus is immediately actionable for investors (earnings, fines, product launches), while non-material news is uniformly low — this dimension largely duplicates materiality without adding discriminating power for META.
